adjudication.method: Expanded adjudicated applicability gold coverage for named authority families and package themes.

adjudication.rationale: Expanded positive applicability gold case for the full named theme set.

cases.package_text:
Purpose and Need

The USDA Forest Service prepares an environmental assessment for a proposed land exchange. The decision notice and FONSI will document the finding of no significant impact. The exchange case includes an agreement to initiate, notice of exchange proposal, appraisal, title evidence, reservations, outstanding rights, equal value, cash equalization, public interest determination, acquisition, disposal, mineral reservation, easement, and exchange approval.

Affected Environment

The project area is on National Forest System land with forest plan direction, management area standards, desired conditions, guidelines, geographic area direction, overlay requirements, species of conservation concern, and a site-specific forest plan amendment. The area includes an inventoried roadless area, recommended wilderness, a wilderness study area, a Wild and Scenic River corridor, a national trail, a special area, cave resource, and paleontological resource. A National Grassland, Bankhead-Jones lands, acquired lands, and grassland plan direction are also described.

Environmental Consequences

The EA evaluates wetlands, waters of the United States, streams, discharge, dredge, fill, stormwater, NPDES, Section 401 water quality certification, Section 404 Corps permit needs, floodplain occupancy, base flood, flood hazard area, air quality, emissions, prescribed fire smoke, general conformity, nonattainment area, state implementation plan, and air quality permit coordination.

Consultation and Resource Topics

The package identifies historic property, cultural resource, archaeological resource, SHPO and THPO consultation, NAGPRA, Native American remains, sacred sites, AIRFA, RFRA, tribal consultation, treaty rights, trust responsibility, government-to-government consultation, cultural confidentiality, listed species, threatened species, endangered species, sensitive species, lynx, biological assessment, biological opinion, Section 7 consultation, habitat, bald eagle, golden eagle, eagle nest, essential fish habitat, EFH, and NOAA Fisheries coordination.

Site Conditions and Project Actions

The record addresses hazardous substance, hazardous waste, CERCLA, RCRA, recognized environmental condition, mine waste, landfill, Phase I environmental site assessment, due diligence, invasive species, noxious weeds, pesticide, herbicide, soil productivity, prime farmland, drinking water, sole source aquifer, watershed, state permit, local permit, minerals, mining, plan of operations, energy development, reclamation, roads, trails, travel management, special use, right-of-way, closure order, timber sale, range permit, grazing, vegetation management, fuels treatment, wildfire, prescribed fire, forest health, insect, disease, restoration, emergency action, and fuel break needs.

adjudication.rationale: Expanded negative applicability gold case for the full named theme set.

cases.package_text:
Purpose and Need

The Forest Service prepares an environmental assessment, but this fixture negates expanded authority-family triggers for coverage testing.

Environmental Consequences

The record states no air quality effects, no wetlands, no waters of the United States, no discharge, no historic properties, no cultural resources, no archaeological resources, no eagle habitat, no eagle nests, no essential fish habitat, no floodplains, no forest plan amendment, not National Grassland, no Bankhead-Jones lands, no hazardous materials, no contamination, no invasive species, no pesticide use, no herbicide use, no prime farmland, no drinking water source, not a land exchange, no appraisal, no title evidence, no exchange agreement, no notice of exchange, no equalization, no acquisition, no disposal, no public interest determination, no mineral activity, no mining, outside National Forest System, no forest plan components, no management area, no road changes, no trail changes, no special use, no right-of-way, no listed species, no sensitive species, no habitat, no consultation required, no tribal consultation, no tribal interests, no sacred sites, no treaty rights, no vegetation treatment, no timber harvest, no fuels treatment, no wildfire action, no wilderness, no designated areas, outside Wild and Scenic River corridor, no national trails, and no caves.

adjudication.rationale: Replayable human adjudication resolves the land-exchange trigger as applicable.

land_exchange_fs_policy_and_project_refe.rationale: Replayable adjudication resolves the scoped weak trigger as applicable.

applicability_adjudication.rationale: Gold adjudication resolves the scoped weak trigger as applicable for replay coverage.

cases.package_text:

The Forest Service prepares an environmental assessment for a small authorization.

Environmental Consequences

A Clean Water Act Section 404 permit may be required if wetlands are confirmed during final design.

cases.package_text:
Purpose and Need

The environmental assessment covers a proposed authorization near a bald eagle nest.

Environmental Consequences

Additional wildlife review may be required if surveys confirm the nest is active.
